node.statement: N1.4 (LIMIT PASSAGE in operator norm). R_F j = j R_Phi, where R_F := (S_F^2)^{-1/2} = lim_N g_N(S_F^2) in A and R_Phi := (S_Phi^2)^{-1/2} = lim_N g_N(S_Phi^2) in B (def-spectral-idempotent). DERIVATION. (i) CONVERGENCE: by GT-taylor-calculus with f(x)=x^{-1/2}, x0=1, applied to X=S_F^2 in A (||S_F^2 - I_A|| = 4||F^2-F|| < 1 by GT-hyp H2, inside radius), the series f(X) is well-defined, i.e. the partial sums g_N(S_F^2) converge IN THE BANACH-ALGEBRA NORM OF A (the operator norm, GT-opnorm) to R_F; identically g_N(S_Phi^2) -> R_Phi in B's norm (||S_Phi^2 - I_B|| = 4||Phi^2-Phi|| < 1). The Taylor tail bound ||g_N(X)-f(X)|| <= sum_{n>N} |a_n| ||X - x0 I||^n -> 0 (a convergent tail of sum |a_n| t^n, t=||X-x0 I||<1) makes the convergence quantitative: set eps_N^F := ||R_F - g_N(S_F^2)|| -> 0 and eps_N^Phi := ||R_Phi - g_N(S_Phi^2)|| -> 0. (ii) ESTIMATE: for every N, R_F j - j R_Phi = (R_F - g_N(S_F^2)) j + (g_N(S_F^2) j - j g_N(S_Phi^2)) + j (g_N(S_Phi^2) - R_Phi). The MIDDLE term vanishes for every N by node 1.3. So R_F j - j R_Phi = (R_F - g_N(S_F^2)) j + j (g_N(S_Phi^2) - R_Phi). Taking operator norm and the triangle inequality: ||R_F j - j R_Phi|| <= ||(R_F - g_N(S_F^2)) j|| + ||j (g_N(S_Phi^2) - R_Phi)|| <= ||R_F - g_N(S_F^2)|| ||j||_op + ||j||_op ||g_N(S_Phi^2) - R_Phi|| [composition bound ||T j|| <= ||T||_op ||j||_op and ||j U|| <= ||j||_op ||U||_op, GT-opnorm; here ||j U|| <= ||j||_op ||U|| also by the op-norm definition applied to the composite] = ||j||_op (eps_N^F + eps_N^Phi). (iii) LIMIT: the left side ||R_F j - j R_Phi|| is a FIXED nonnegative number independent of N, while the right side ||j||_op (eps_N^F + eps_N^Phi) -> 0 as N->infinity (||j||_op < infinity finite by GT-hyp H3; eps_N -> 0 by (i)). A fixed number <= a sequence tending to 0 must be <= 0, hence = 0. Therefore R_F j = j R_Phi. Uses node 1.3, GT-taylor-calculus, GT-opnorm, GT-hyp, def-spectral-idempotent.
